text:
Unma Hui Guo Rou (ウンマ＝ホイコーロ, Unma Hoikōro) is the First Queen of the Kakin Empire's eight Queens and first wife of Nasubi Hui Guo Rou. She gave him three sons, 1 st Prince Benjamin Hui Guo Rou, and 4 th Prince Tserriednich Hui Guo Rou, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 349 and 9 th Prince Halkenburg Hui Guo Rou, though for an unknown reason had Second Queen Duazul raise him as her own son. It is rumored on international websites that the reason is to protect Halkenburg from assassination plots from his older siblings. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 403 

Appearance

Unma's physical appearance is first seen in a portrait of her, shown in an overview of the princes with their mothers and guards. In this portrait, Unma has short, blonde hair, a rectangular face shape, a wide, flat nose, and fair skin. 

Unma's first appearance in the story is as a silhouette when the 14 Princes are first introduced as one of the eight queens. However, the hairstyle she had then does not line up with her later portrait, indicating that her design may not have been finalized then.

In her first physical appearance, Unma wears a frilly dress as she speaks on the phone with Halkenburg. She has neatly combed-back fair hair that ends near her shoulders, fashioned into roughly a bob. She has a rectangular face shape, a wide flat nose, and fair skin. 

Personality

Not much is known about Unma's personality. She appears to be a supportive and understanding mother, expressing her support and pride for Halkenburg to him over a choice he makes which would mean he could not see her at the banquet on Day 15. 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

Queen Unma's name is first mentioned as mother of 1 st Prince Benjamin and 4 th Prince Tserriednich when Kakin's 14 Princes are first revealed, and is the first of King Nasubi's 8 wives. She is pictured as a silhouette, the tallest among the 8 queens. 

Shortly after the departure ceremony, an unknown attacker kills five of Woble's guards. As a result, Kurapika uses his Dowsing Chain on the remaining guards to discern their true affiliations, and identifies two unnamed guards who are not truly affiliated with Kurapika's employer Oito, one of whom mentions that the seven guards (comprising the two of them and the five deceased) were each sent by a higher queen. The guards are restrained and divulge the Queens' espionage system to Kurapika, Oito, and Woble's other remaining guard to explain themselves, and are murdered shortly after by Sayird after he falls victim to manipulation by Momoze's Guardian Spirit Beast. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 360 

After a meeting Kurapika, 8 th Queen Oito, and 14 th Prince Woble have with 3 rd Prince Zhang Lei, Queen Unma contacts and orders Coventoba to inform Slakka to become Woble's bodyguard along with Hashito and Sakata. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 366 

A picture of Queen Unma is shown as being the mother to both Benjamin and Tserriednich. She is stated to have 9 guards accessible to both Benjamin and Tserriednich. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 374 

Unma's man Wolfe appears with Furykov to intercept Camilla in the corridor of between Rooms 1001 and 1002 when she goes to Room 1001 to attack Benjamin. Wolfe is killed in the exchange by a shot from Camilla to the head. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 373 Wolfe is later mentioned in the trial between Camilla and Benjamin. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 376 

On the eleventh day of the Voyage, Unma receives a call from 9 th Prince Halkenburg in her room. She supports him, telling him that what he told her sounded like the kind of choice he would make, then expresses her sorrow at being unable to see him at the next banquet, before letting him know of the pride she had in him, revealing him as her son. 

In the early morning of the eleventh day, Fugetsu makes her way around Tier 1 to pass a letter that Kacho had written to each Prince, secretly containing scandalous information that the Justice Bureau had gathered and Kaiser had suggested leaking to each Prince. Benjamin reads his letter, which reveals that Unma is Halkenburg's mother, and mentions that internet rumors on international websites theorize that Unma had asked a secret favor of Duazul to protect Halkenburg from assassination plots by his older siblings by changing his order in line to the throne. Kacho writes that although the format of the succession battle had made the order of succession essentially meaningless, if the rumors were true, Unma might be using her power to pull the strings for Halkenburg. 

Unma's Royal Guards

 Active/Alive • Deceased • Former/Arrested 

 • • 

Abilities & Powers

As the first queen, Unma has the authority to send her own soldiers to spy on the lesser princes. The first queen also has authority over Benjamin's men, being able to give Coventoba orders through Mandam. Finally, Unma also has authority over some of 2 nd Queen Duazul's men owing to a collaboration between them, where the men write up surveillance reports on the younger princes. 

Manga Appearances

Trivia

Every queen of Kakin has a numerical theme on their names which corresponds to their marital order. "Un" means "one" in French.
Two of Unma’s sons, Tserridnich and Halkenburg, have Germanic elements to their names:
The “ie” in Tserriednich’s name is represented by an i-row kana and a chōonpu (a mark looking similar to a dash that lengthens the preceding vowel sound) in the original Japanese text, indicating that the intended pronunciation is that represented in the IPA as /iː/ (like the “ee” in the English word “meet”), which is how the “ie” letter combination is pronounced in German.
The “ch” digraph at the end, as well as the intended H-like pronunciation (implied by the use of an H-column kana in the original Japanese text of his name) is also a feature of German.
The ending “-burg” (spelling varies by language) is also a Germanic morpheme signifying a city or fortified place.
